fields.description:
## Why
Turn open-ended assumption checking into an enumerable probe-each scan, and add a cheap early hedge signal -- all feeding WS1's committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim.

## What (with review-surfaced corrections)
- G-7(a): give plan_review_E4.md a confident-assertion trigger lexicon as its SCAN PROTOCOL. The lexicon MUST be enumerated in the prompt (assertion-shaped verb/frame list); E4 already says "scan for assertions" so this sharpens, not replaces.
- G-4: E4 sub-check for scope-exclusion / descoping claims, at NORMAL priority. Discrimination rule co-located in the rubric: fire on empirically-checkable (codebase) exclusions; abstain-with-coverage for external-fact exclusions. Add an AC for the abstain branch.
- G-7(b) exec:DET hedge detector -- OPEN DESIGN ISSUE surfaced by review, do NOT treat as settled: (1) the existing exec:DET mechanism (det_invariants.py, Engine B via opengrep/ast-grep) scans CODE FILES, not plan-prose strings; (2) DET-tier findings BYPASS Pass 2 (they merge straight into partition_findings), so they do NOT "flow through Pass 2". Resolve BEFORE building: either add a prose-scanning DET path that is explicitly routed INTO the verifier, or implement the hedge signal as a 1-TURN finder rather than exec:DET. Dedup vs E6.no_hedges (which is scoped to ACs using hedges instead of proving commands) = same-location suppression.
- committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim is INTRODUCED BY WS1 (sibling), not pre-existing -- this story depends on WS1.

## Provenance
Labels like G-8 / FP-3a / R-6 / WS-N are provenance tags from the DSO gap-report evaluation (~/dso_rebar_plan_review_gap_report.md) + session log brief-bride-rash (95f1-1b3b-a867-4020) -- they are NOT codebase symbols and cannot be grepped. Concrete code targets are named inline and in this ticket's file_impact.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] E4 confident-assertion lexicon enumerated in plan_review_E4.md. Proof: `grep -c .` shows the phrase list; add recall case in tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py -k confident_assertion.
- [ ] E4 scope-exclusion sub-check (normal priority) with an explicit codebase-vs-external discrimination rule AND an abstain-with-coverage AC for external-fact exclusions.
- [ ] Hedge-detector mechanism DECIDED and documented (exec:DET-into-verifier vs 1-TURN finder), reconciling the "DET scans code / DET bypasses Pass 2" facts. Proof: design note in ticket + the chosen path has a recall test.
- [ ] Dedup vs E6.no_hedges specified (same-location suppression) and tested.
- [ ] depends_on WS1 recorded.
